subject: Re: Template matching giving flat CC volumes and no results
body:
Hi Alister,

the memory consumption depends on the Y dimension of the full tomogram. 
Matching the tilt series you sent me before at 10 A/px, the memory 
consumption peaks at around 7 GB. You can decrease the memory footprint by 
matching at a larger pixel size.

There are plans to rewrite the template matching procedure for tilt series 
to make it faster and less memory-intensive, but this will take some time.

Cheers,
Dimitry


On Tuesday, December 10, 2019 at 6:01:10 PM UTC+1, Alister Burt wrote:
>
> Hi all,
>
> I was looking to test out the template matching routine today and ran into 
> a problem, the process seems to run fine (the nice animation shows up) but 
> CC volumes which are output are completely flat and the resulting star file 
> thus contains no particles.
>
> Some info...
> My tomograms are not inverted (protein is dark), my reference is the same. 
> Could this cause a problem?
> I tried with and without spectral whitening at various angular samplings 
> and with a few different symmetries, all gave the same result.
>
> Any ideas?
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
>
